name: WWKTM
description: Website for WWKTM, a two-day international conference on web technologies and everything around the web. Use the provided logo, match its vibes and colors, and research WWKTM plus related events like Bar Code Camp, AI Conf, and Tech Kura Kani online, including extracting their logos.
personaStyleGuide: Build the site like a modern cultural-tech festival rather than a corporate developer conference. The visual anchor is folded summit chevrons: angular, ribbon-like shapes inspired by the provided WWKTM mark, used as dividers, masks, card corners, and directional motifs. Keep the interface bright and breathable, with a strong sense of altitude, clarity, and movement. Typography should feel editorial and international: bold condensed or semi-condensed display headlines paired with a highly legible neo-grotesk body. Use oversized headlines, generous top margins, and asymmetrical composition. Cards should feel tactile but not glassy: soft solid surfaces, subtle 1px tinted borders, and short, crisp shadows. Avoid generic SaaS gradients; instead use controlled color blocking with glacier blue and ember orange taken from the logo. Motion should feel purposeful and event-like: marquee topic rails, staggered reveal of speakers, sliding schedule tracks, and diagonal wipe transitions based on the logo geometry. Photography and imagery should prioritize real community moments, speakers, Kathmandu context, and candid conversations. The mood is inclusive, design-literate, and quietly ambitious. Spacing should be airy, with strong vertical rhythm and alternating full-bleed and contained sections. Use section labels, date/location metadata, and sharp iconography to create a premium conference editorial system.
features: Two-day schedule explorer with track filtering and day-based narrative flow
Speaker grid with expandable bios, topic tags, and global-local highlights
Community ecosystem strip connecting WWKTM, BarCamp Kathmandu, AI Conf, and Tech Kura Kani
Ticketing and attendance conversion blocks with clear urgency states
Sponsor and partner showcase framed as part of a broader web community movement

sections.title: Hero Summit
sections.description: Introduce WWKTM as a two-day international conference on web technologies and everything around the web, with date, Kathmandu context, and a strong brand statement that positions it as both global and community-rooted.
sections.layoutHint: Full-bleed editorial hero with oversized type on the left, folded-chevron logo composition on the right, and layered metadata chips for dates, location, and ticket state.

sections.title: Why WWKTM
sections.description: Explain the conference mission: connecting web technologists, designers, builders, and internet thinkers through talks, collaboration, and shared perspective from Kathmandu to the world.
sections.layoutHint: Asymmetrical two-column story layout with pull quotes, stat blocks, and angled section separators derived from the logo geometry.

sections.title: Two-Day Format
sections.description: Present the event structure clearly as a two-day experience, balancing talks, community interaction, and the distinctive WWKTM social experience around the web ecosystem.
sections.layoutHint: Horizontal timeline with alternating day cards, each using color-blocked panels and subtle motion on hover.

sections.title: Speaker Lineup
sections.description: Feature international and regional speakers across frontend, backend, design, AI-on-the-web, product thinking, developer tooling, security, and internet culture.
sections.layoutHint: Modular portrait grid with expandable speaker drawers, topic ribbons, and editorial image cropping.

sections.title: Web Tracks
sections.description: Break down the programming into thematic tracks such as frontend craft, platform engineering, design systems, AI for the web, developer experience, and open internet topics.
sections.layoutHint: Bento-grid taxonomy cards with angular corners, each card carrying an icon, track description, and sample session themes.

sections.title: Community Network
sections.description: Show WWKTM as the flagship node in a wider event ecosystem, referencing related initiatives like BarCamp Kathmandu, AI Conf, and Tech Kura Kani as proof of a living year-round tech community.
sections.layoutHint: Connected brand rail with logo tiles, short event summaries, and animated link-lines that make the ecosystem feel intentional and alive.

sections.title: Schedule Preview
sections.description: Give attendees a fast way to understand flow, pacing, and key moments across both conference days without overwhelming them.
sections.layoutHint: Scrollable schedule board styled like a premium event newspaper meets transit display, with sticky time column and colored session markers.

sections.title: Venue And City
sections.description: Frame Kathmandu as part of the experience, emphasizing place, hospitality, and the uniqueness of a global web conference in Nepal.
sections.layoutHint: Editorial split with venue info on one side and immersive city/community photography on the other, overlaid with directional chevron motifs.

sections.title: Partners And Sponsors
sections.description: Present sponsors and community supporters as collaborators enabling knowledge exchange, local growth, and international connection.
sections.layoutHint: Clean monochrome logo wall on white surfaces, with selected sponsor stories elevated in accent-colored callout cards.

sections.title: Tickets And Updates
sections.description: Drive registration, newsletter signups, and community follow-through with clear states for early bird, sold out, or waitlist scenarios.
sections.layoutHint: High-contrast conversion section with stacked pricing cards, strong CTA buttons, and a compact signup module framed by orange and blue angular bands.

branding.aesthetic: Editorial tech festival with alpine clarity, folded geometric forms, bright surfaces, and a premium yet approachable conference feel rooted in Kathmandu.

imagery.style: Documentary-style conference photography mixed with crisp geometric illustration and logo-led motion graphics
